Basic Safety Precautions. This section warns against fire, electric shock, or injury.  It details grounding requirements for permanently connected dishwashers and cautions against excessive downward force on the open dishwasher door before installation.

Electrical Safety. This section emphasizes the importance of grounding the dishwasher and adhering to specific electrical guidelines. It warns against using extension cords, improper installation locations, and handling damaged power cords.  It specifies the required voltage and frequency (120V AC, 60Hz) and highlights the need for qualified electrician intervention for installation, repair, and troubleshooting.

Before using the dishwasher, please read the Important Safety Instructions. Water supply temperature should be a minimum of 120 °F (49 °C) for better washing and reasonable cycle time. For this reason, use the hot water supply only. It is also important that the dishwasher is loaded according to instructions for effective cleaning. When loading the dishwasher, make sure that insoluble items such as bones, toothpicks, and pieces of paper have been removed.

When loading the dishwasher, observe the following general guidelines: 1. Place items with deep dish bottoms at an angle to help water run off and speed the drying process. 2. Remove solid, burned-on scraps from dishes prior to loading in the dishwasher. 3. Only load items that are dishwasher-safe.
NOTE Do not load utensils that are not heat-resistant, have decorative metal trim, etc.

SPECIFICATIONS Power Supply: 120 V, 60 Hz AC only, minimum 15 A circuit breaker Water Pressure: 20–120 psi (140–830 kPa) Size (WxDxH): 23 3/4" x 24 5/8" x 33 1/2" (603 mm x 625 mm x 851 mm) Inlet Water Temperature: 120 °F (49 °C) minimum Product Weight: - LDF8874: 100.5 lbs. (45.6 kg) - LDF8764: 92.1 lbs. (41.8 kg) - LDF7774: 96.6 lbs. (43.8 kg) - LDS5774: 94.9 lbs. (43.1 kg) The appearance and specifications are subject to change without notice.

Caution: Empty the lower rack first to avoid water dripping from the upper rack onto dishes in the lower rack.

Warning: Do not open the door while the dishwasher is in operation. If opened, a safety device will stop the dishwasher. Care should be taken when opening the door immediately following the cycle as steam may escape.
Auto-off: The dishwasher will automatically turn off if a cycle is not selected or if the door is not closed within four minutes

This section contains the title "LOADING THE LOWER RACK" and a caution alert. It also lists items that are not safe for the dishwasher, such as hand-painted silver or gold leaf dishes, wood or bone-handled utensils, iron or other rust-prone utensils, and plastic items that are not dishwasher safe.  It advises checking manufacturer's recommendations when in doubt.

This section describes the extra tall tub's capacity for loading dishes, pans, or other utensils up to 14 inches high.  It recommends the lower rack for dinner plates, soup bowls, dishes, pots, pans, lids, and grill trays, explaining the correct positioning for optimal cleaning.  The use of the silverware basket in either rack is also mentioned.
This is an image showing a suggested pattern for loading dishes in the lower rack of the dishwasher.

This section provides tips for loading the rack, such as loading plates and bowls between the tines, and loading large items to the sides or back to prevent blocking water spray to the detergent dispenser. It also warns against obstructing the spray arms rotation with large items.

This section describes the recommendations for using the lower rack.  It explains that the lower rack should not be used for small items like cups, wine glasses, or small plates and that these should be loaded into the upper rack.

This section specifies loading instructions for models with steam, advising the placement of heavily soiled or stained dishes in the front-left area of the lower rack with the dirty side down to maximize the cleaning performance.

Note: When loading larger items in the upper rack, such as wine glasses or taller cups, it may be necessary to remove the cutlery rack.

The upper rack adjusts up and down. Adjust the upper rack up to allow tall and oversized items of up to 14 inches high in the lower rack. Adjust the upper rack down to allow placement of large dishes of up to 12 inches high in the upper rack. The upper rack comes in the upper position.
Illustration of the upper rack in the dishwasher, showing how to lower it.
To lower the rack: Hold the rack at the center of each side to support its weight. Then, lift the lever on each side to release the rack and lower it to the lower position.
Illustration of the upper rack in the dishwasher, showing how to raise it.

To raise the rack: Simply lift the rack at the center of each side (see illustration) until the rack locks into place in the upper position. It is not necessary to lift the lock levers to raise the rack.
Note: After adjusting, make sure that dishes in the upper and lower racks do not interfere with the rotation of the spray arm. Do not adjust the rack while it is loaded with dishes.

Note: Remove the label from the dispenser before use.
Note: Don't add detergent when using the Rinse Only cycle.
Note: Too much detergent can leave a film on dishes and in the dishwasher, resulting in poor wash performance. Don't use detergent tablets for shorter cycles, as they may not fully dissolve.
Caution: Use only detergent designed for automatic dishwashers. Incorrect detergent may cause excessive suds, reduce wash performance, and lead to leaks. Avoid hand dishwashing detergent.

Title: CARE & CLEANING.  A short paragraph about periodically checking and caring for the spray arms, followed by a warning to wear gloves or protective clothing during care and maintenance/cleaning.

Title: CLEANING THE FILTERS. A numbered list of five steps for cleaning the filters.  It involves removing the lower rack, turning the mesh filter counterclockwise, taking out all three filters, cleaning them if necessary, and replacing them in reverse order.
An image showing the location of the three filters within the dishwasher.
Individual images of the three filters (mesh, outer, upper STS) with labels.

A note about cleaning the filter after each load to prevent food debris and odor. Also, to ensure correct assembly of the filter to avoid poor cleaning results. If hard water is present, it recommends cleaning the filter at least once a month.
Information on cleaning all three filters according to the chart recommendations. Use non-abrasive materials and warm running water. A soft brush may be needed.
Caution: mentions a soft brush may be needed for the upper STS filter and to be cautious of sharp edges when handling the filter.

Columns include Number of Loads Per Week, If you wash dishes before loading, If you scrape and rinse dishes before loading, If you only scrape dishes before loading, and If you do not scrape or rinse dishes before loading.

Instructions on seasonal maintenance and storage of a dishwasher. Includes steps on closing the water valve, unplugging the power cord, cleaning the filter, tub and dispenser, and winterizing the water supply lines and the dishwasher itself.

Is a rinse aid required to be used? Rinse aid is strongly recommended to enhance the drying performance of the dishwasher. In order to be more energy efficient, the hybrid drying system does not utilize a heating element in order to assist in drying, so a rinse aid plays an integral role in the drying process. If a rinse aid is not used, water may remain on the dishes and the tub. Rinse aid will help to ensure that the majority of the water has been sheeted off of the dishes at the end of the rinse and that the load is ready for the dry cycle.

What can be done if my dishes have a cloudy, white film on them after a wash? The cloudy film is usually caused by minerals in the water reacting with the dishwasher detergent. First, be certain not to use too much detergent. Refer to the owner's manual for recommended detergent amounts by cycle. Add an Extra Rinse to the cycle, or run a Heavy cycle. LG Dishwashers use less water than traditional dishwashers, so adding an Extra Rinse will aid in rinsing the film away. If the water supply is hard, it may be necessary to add a performance booster that addresses the hard water condition.
What is the Sanitary Rinse and how is it activated? The Sanitary Rinse will increase the heated rinse to approximately 161°F (72°C). The normal heated rinse temperature will vary from cycle to cycle.

What steps can I take to improve drying results? Drying performance is directly affected by the amount of heat that remains in the tub after the heated rinse is performed. If your dishwasher is far away from your home's hot water heater, it may be necessary to run the hot water tap in the sink before starting a wash cycle, to ensure that cool water is purged from the hot water lines. This will ensure your dishwasher has a consistent supply of hot water from start to finish. Using the Sanitary Rinse setting will also aid in keeping the inner drum temperature and dishes hotter before drying begins. LG dishwashers are designed to be used in conjunction with a rinse aid. Using a rinse aid will maximize the amount of water that has already been sheeted away from the dishes before the dry cycle begins. If a rinse aid is used and the performance still needs a boost, adjust the amount of rinse aid used by the indicator on the rinse aid cap. 4 is the default setting, but if necessary, set the level to 5 so more rinse aid will be used. Allow the dishwasher to completely finish the dry cycle before opening the door. If the door is opened mid dry cycle, the heat that is necessary for the hybrid dry system to work will escape. This will cause all of the dishes inside to remain wet once the cycle finishes.

What is the hybrid drying system? The dishwasher does not rely on a heating element for the drying process; instead, it relies on the heat that is retained by the dishes and the tub during the wash and rinse cycles to turn the moisture in the tub into humidity. Warm, moist air in the dishwasher is then pulled into a condensing duct in the door by a fan. The air then cools, which causes the humidity to condense. Room temperature air is also drawn into the duct to help the air cool down faster. When this moisture condenses into a liquid, it is drained out.
NOTE Rinse aid is required to enhance the drying performance. If no rinse aid is used, water may remain on the dishes and the tub.

Why is there a whistling sound? It is important to determine how long the noise occurs and when it happens (what part of the cycle). When water is drawn into the dishwasher, a whistling noise can be heard. This is normal. If the water pressure is too high, it will be louder so it may be necessary to adjust the water valve to lower the pressure if the noise is too loud.

What is Dual Control? Dual Control is an option that can be utilized during wash cycles. This option enables you to fully customize the wash cycle intensity for the upper and lower racks, independently, in the same load. For example, by selecting a soft spray for the upper rack and strong spray for the lower rack, you can wash delicate stemware in the upper rack while washing pots and pans in the lower rack.

What kind of dishes can be placed in the dishwasher? Ensure that the dishes you place in the dishwasher are dishwasher safe. Plastic dishware will not dry as effectively as glass and metal dishware. Plastic items do not retain heat as well as glass and metal dishware. Plastic cups and dishes may require some hand drying before being put away at the end of the cycle.

Can I perform a drain only cycle? The dishwasher does not have a drain only function; however, the dishwasher will drain on its own for the first 45 seconds of a new cycle. To drain standing water, select a wash cycle and let it run for only 45 seconds. Then, turn the unit off and let it reset. Repeat as necessary.
This section describes how long the clean indicator light stays on after a cycle is complete. It mentions that it stays lit for four minutes but can be programmed to stay on until the door is opened.
This section explains why the dishwasher beeps continuously if the door is opened during or after a cycle. It states that this is normal and is a warning that the interior temperatures are hot.

This section describes the benefit of steam in dishwashers. It mentions that it allows for thorough cleaning, uses less water than other methods, enhances cleaning performance for fine china and stemware, and helps in melting oily or waxy substances.

This section explains the child lock feature. It describes how it prevents controls from being changed during a cycle but does not prevent the door from being opened or the unit from being powered off.  It also explains how to activate or deactivate it.
This section explains the meaning of "PF" appearing on the display.  It clarifies that it doesn't indicate a malfunction but rather a prior power failure.
This section specifies the size of the 90° elbow needed for installation; it should have a 3/8" NPT connection to the dishwasher.
This section answers the question about adding an extension to the drain hose; no, it's not possible.

This section contains frequently asked questions and answers about the product, such as the necessity of using rinse aid, troubleshooting cloudy or white film on dishes after washing, explanation of the hygienic rinse function, steps to improve drying results, and the description of the hybrid drying system and Double Control option.
Frequently Asked Questions about dishwashers

What type of dishes can be put in the dishwasher? Make sure the dishes are dishwasher safe. Plastic dishes don't dry as well as glass or metal dishes. Plastic doesn't retain heat as well, so plastic cups and dishes may need to be hand-dried before putting away.

Can I only run a drying cycle? The dishwasher doesn't have a drying-only cycle; however, it will drain for 45 seconds at the beginning of any cycle. To drain standing water, select a wash cycle and let it run for 45 seconds. Then turn it off and on again. Repeat as needed.

How long does the cleaning indicator stay on after the cycle is complete? The cleaning indicator light stays on for 4 minutes before turning off. You can also set it to stay on until the door is opened.

Why does my dishwasher beep continuously if I open the door during or after a cycle? This is normal. The dishwasher is letting you know that the inside is hot and you should be careful when reaching inside. Once it cools down, the beeping will stop.
Product Characteristics and Operation

What are the benefits of using steam? Steam allows for thorough cleaning of burnt food with less water than other methods. It also improves cleaning of fine china and other delicate dishes. Steam particles are smaller and more active, so they penetrate better and help melt greasy or waxy substances, like lipstick on the rim of glasses.

What is the child lock and how do I activate it? The child lock prevents changes to the settings during a cycle; it doesn't prevent the door from opening or the unit from turning off. To activate or deactivate it, press the Spray and Rinse buttons simultaneously for 3 seconds.

Why does "PF" appear on the screen? PF doesn't indicate a malfunction. It means there was a power failure. This feature protects the dishwasher in case of power outages. When power is restored and a button is pressed, the message will remain for 10 seconds, then disappear.
Installation
What size is the 90° elbow? The 90° elbow should have 3/8" NPT threads on the end that connects to the dishwasher.
Can I add an extension to the drain hose? No, extensions cannot be added.

Title of the document: informations importantes de sécurité.  Safety instructions before use. Your safety and that of others is very important. We provide many important safety tips in this manual and on your appliance. Always read all safety messages and follow them.
Warning symbol: a triangle with an exclamation mark inside.
This is a danger symbol. This symbol alerts you to potential hazards that may result in the destruction of the appliance or serious injury or death.
The danger symbol will be followed by the word "AVERTISSEMENT" or "DANGER" and then an important safety message. These words mean:
AVERTISSEMENT: indicates the risk of serious injury or death.
DANGER: indicates the risk of injury or damage to the appliance.
Precautions for use. To reduce the risk of fire, electric shock, or injury during operation of the appliance, follow the precautions for use, including the following:

This manual contains very important information regarding the installation, use and maintenance of your dishwasher. The manufacturer cannot be held responsible in case of injury or damage caused by non-compliance with these safety instructions.
Carefully read all instructions before using the dishwasher.
Do not use the dishwasher for anything other than its intended purpose.

Title: Precautions for Use.  This section contains warnings about fire, electric shock, and injury risks. It advises connecting the dishwasher to a grounded system or using an isolation cable.  It also warns against excessive force on the door during installation to avoid damage or tipping.

Title: Electrical Safety. This section emphasizes electrical safety and lists warnings about electric shock and death risks. It mandates grounding the dishwasher, prohibits using an extension cord, specifies the power requirements and safety measures, and warns about improper installation or storage.

This section details safe use and storage practices, such as avoiding placing heavy objects on the power cord.  It also prohibits use if the power cord or plug is damaged.  Wet hands and using the appliance with damaged components are strongly discouraged. Repair or disassembling should only be performed by qualified professionals.

Table recommending filter cleaning frequency based on the number of washes per week and pre-wash cleaning practices.  Columns: Number of washes per week, cleaning if plates are washed before loading, cleaning if plates are rinsed and scraped before loading, cleaning if plates are lightly scraped before loading, cleaning if plates are neither rinsed nor scraped before loading.

Cleaning the interior of the dishwasher: Regularly clean the interior with a damp sponge or soft cloth to remove food residue and dirt particles. To remove odors and white stains, run an empty cycle with detergent or a cup of white vinegar in a dishwasher-safe bowl in the top basket. (Vinegar is acidic, so avoid overuse to prevent damage.)

Cleaning the exterior of the dishwasher: Typically, the exterior can be cleaned with a damp sponge or soft cloth and dried with a dry cloth. For stainless steel exteriors, use a suitable cleaning product.

Cleaning the lower wash arm: Ensure the spray holes are not blocked by food particles. If cleaning is necessary, remove the lower wash arm by pulling it upwards. After cleaning, replace it, ensuring it clicks into place and rotates freely.
A grayscale image showing how to remove the lower wash arm.

Cleaning the upper wash arm: Pull the upper basket forward. Ensure the spray holes are not clogged with food particles. If cleaning is required, remove the upper wash arm by turning the nut on its base one-eighth of a turn counterclockwise and pushing it in.
A grayscale image showing how to remove the upper wash arm.
After cleaning the upper wash arm, press it back into place. Turn the nut one-eighth of a turn clockwise to secure it.
A diagram showing how to remove the top wash arm
A diagram showing how to replace the top wash arm
Instructions in French on how to clean the top wash arm.  Includes steps for removing and replacing the arm, and notes on using a screwdriver if needed.
Seasonal maintenance and storage instructions in French.  Recommends turning off water and power during warm months and winterizing the unit during cold months.
Questions and answers about the product
Should I use a rinse aid? A rinse aid is highly recommended to improve the drying performance of the dishwasher.

What can I do if my dishes are covered with a cloudy white film after washing? The cloudy film is usually caused by the reaction of minerals in the water with the dishwasher detergent. Make sure you do not use too much detergent. Refer to the owner's manual for the recommended amount of detergent per cycle. Add an extra rinse cycle. Add an intensive cycle.
What is the sanitizing rinse and how do I activate it? The sanitizing rinse increases the hot rinse to about 161°F (72°C). The normal temperature of the hot rinse varies depending on the cycle.

What steps can I take to improve drying? Drying is directly affected by the amount of heat remaining in the tank after the hot rinse operation. If your dishwasher is far from your boiler, you may need to run hot water in the sink to start a wash cycle, to ensure that cold water is removed from the pipes. This will ensure that your dishwasher receives hot water from start to finish.

LG dishwashers are designed to be used with a rinse aid. The cleaning product maximizes the amount of water already drained from the dishes before the drying cycle begins. If you use a rinse aid and the performance is not satisfactory, adjust the amount of rinse aid sent using the arrow on the rinse aid dispenser cover. The default setting is 4, but if necessary, set it to 5 to use more rinse aid.

What is the hybrid drying system? The dishwasher does not rely on a heating element for drying; instead, it relies on the heat retained by the dishes and the tank during the washing and rinsing cycles to convert the tank mist into moisture. The hot, humid air from the dishwasher is then discharged by a fan to a condensation pipe in the door. The air cools, causing moisture to condense. Outside air is also ventilated into the pipe to accelerate cooling.

Why is there a whistling sound? It is important to determine the duration of the sound and when it occurs (which part of the cycle). When water is sent to the dishwasher, a whistling sound can occur. This is normal. If the water pressure is too high, it will be louder, and it may be necessary to adjust the water inlet valve to reduce the pressure if the sound is too loud.

What is the Multi Wash cycle? Multi Wash is an option that can be activated during wash cycles. This option allows you to customize the intensity of the wash cycle for the upper and lower baskets independently, in the same program.

What types of dishes can be put in the dishwasher? Make sure that the dishes you put in the dishwasher are suitable for this. Plastic dishes do not dry as effectively as glass and metal dishes. Plastic dishes do not retain heat as effectively as glass and metal dishes.
Questions and answers about a dishwasher

Can I run only a purge cycle? The dishwasher does not have a purge function, but it drains automatically for 45 seconds during a new cycle. To remove stagnant water, run a cycle for 45 seconds and then turn it off to reset it.
How long does the CLEAN indicator stay on after the cycle ends? It remains on for 4 minutes, but it can be programmed to stay on until the door is opened.
Why does the dishwasher beep continuously if I open the door during or after a cycle? It is normal. The dishwasher is warning you that the interior is very hot.
What is the advantage of steam? It provides a deeper clean, uses less water than scrubbing, and is gentler on delicate items.

What is the Child Lock and how do I activate it? It prevents changes to controls during the cycle, but not turning off the machine or opening the door. It is activated by simultaneously pressing the spray and rinse buttons for three seconds.
Why is "PF" displayed on the screen? It does not indicate a malfunction. It indicates a power failure.
Installation: What size is the 90-degree elbow? It must be a NPT 3/8" to connect to the dishwasher.
Installation: Can I add an extension to the drain hose? No, this is not allowed.
